GPCAMERA_NETWORK_CONNECT_NEW_AP.name: Connect to a New Access Point

5.name: Video Timelapse Rate
5.description: How frequently to take a video when performing a Video Timelapse

30.name: Photo Timelapse Rate
30.description: How frequently to take a photo when performing a Photo Timelapse.

32.name: Nightlapse Rate
32.description:
How frequently to take a video or photo when performing a Nightlapse.

This controls the Video or Photo Nightlapse rate if Setting 128 is set to 21 or 26 respectively.

168.name: Scheduled Capture
168.description:
Configure or disable the scheduled capture functionality to start encoding at a future time.

This is a bit-masked value with the following bitwise definitions,
numbered from least significant bit, with the example showing parsed fields from a sample of `0x00000c8b`.

| Bit(s) | Definition                    | Example |
| ------ | ----------------------------- | ------- |
| 0      | Is Scheduled Capture Enabled? | 1       |
| 1      | Is 24 hour format?            | 1       |
| 2-8    | Minute                        | 34      |
| 9-13   | Hour                          | 12      |
| 14-63  | Reserved                      | 0       |

Note that when the scheduled capture time occurs, encoding will be started and continue indefinitely.  One
of the duration settings (156, 157, 172) can be used to set the encoding duration depending on the camera mode.

88.name: LCD Brightness
88.description: The LCD brightness as a percentage value from 10-100

236.name: Automatic Wi-Fi Access Point
236.description: Configure the camera's wifi access point to automatically start on boot of the camera

237.name: Auto Power On USB
237.description: Setting to automatically power on when the camera is connected to a power source.

1.name: Battery Present
1.description: Is the system's internal battery present? This can be false when the camera is powered by an external source without a battery inserted.

2.name: Internal Battery Bars
2.description: Rough approximation of internal battery level in bars (or charging). When the battery is in any charging state, the value is always 4 (Charging) regardless of the current bar count. For a more precise reading, use [Internal Battery Percentage](/docs/ble/statuses#status-70) (Internal Battery Percentage).

13.name: Video Encoding Duration
13.description: When encoding video, this is the duration (seconds) of the video so far; 0 otherwise

20.name: Last Pairing Type
20.description: The last type of pairing in which the camera was engaged

21.name: Last Pairing Success
21.description: Time since boot (milliseconds) of last successful pairing complete action

22.name: Wifi Scan State
22.description: State of current scan for WiFi Access Points

23.name: Last Wifi Scan Success
23.description: Time since boot (milliseconds) that the WiFi Access Point scan completed

27.name: Remote Connected
27.description: Is a wireless remote control connected?

28.name: Pairing State (Legacy)
28.description: Wireless Pairing State. Each bit contains state information (see WirelessPairingStateFlags)

29.name: Connected WiFi SSID
29.description:
The name of the wireless network that the camera is connected to where the camera is acting as a client/station.

When read via BLE, this value is big-endian byte-encoded int32.

30.name: Access Point SSID
30.description:
The name of the network that the camera sets up in AP mode for other devices to connect to.

When read via BLE, this value is big-endian byte-encoded int32.

31.name: Connected Devices
31.description: The number of wireless devices connected to the camera

32.name: Preview Stream
32.description: Is the camera's preview stream currently active? When true (1), the camera is sending MPEG-TS data via UDP to the client that started the stream. Use [Preview Stream Available](/docs/ble/statuses#status-55) to check whether starting a preview stream is possible in the current mode before sending a start request.

34.name: Remaining Photos
34.description:
How many photos can be taken with current settings before sdcard is full.

Alternatively, this is:

- the remaining timelapse capability if Setting 128 is set to Timelapse Photo
- the remaining nightlapse capability if Setting 128 is set to Nightlapse Photo

35.name: Remaining Video Time
35.description:
How many seconds of video can be captured with current settings before sdcard is full

Alternatively, this is:

- the remaining timelapse capability if Setting 128 is set to Timelapse Video
- the remaining nightlapse capability if Setting 128 is set to Nightlapse Video

38.name: Photos
38.description: Total number of photos on sdcard

39.name: Videos
39.description: Total number of videos on sdcard

41.name: OTA
41.description: The current status of Over The Air (OTA) update

42.name: Pending FW Update Cancel
42.description: Is there a pending request to cancel a firmware update download?

49.name: Timelapse Interval Countdown
49.description: The current timelapse interval countdown value (e.g. 5...4...3...2...1...)

54.name: SD Card Remaining
54.description: Remaining space on the sdcard in Kilobytes

55.name: Preview Stream Available
55.description: Is preview stream supported in the current recording/mode/secondary-stream? Check this status before sending a start preview stream request. If false (0), the camera will not be able to start a preview stream in its current state. See also [Preview Stream](/docs/ble/statuses#status-32) which indicates whether a stream is currently active.

58.name: Active Hilights
58.description: The number of hilights in currently-encoding video (value is set to 0 when encoding stops)

59.name: Time Since Last Hilight
59.description: Time since boot (milliseconds) of most recent hilight in encoding video (set to 0 when encoding stops)

60.name: Minimum Status Poll Period
60.description:
The minimum time between camera status updates (milliseconds). Best practice is to not poll for status more
often than this

68.name: GPS Lock
68.description: Does the camera currently have a GPS lock?

69.name: AP Mode
69.description:
Indicates whether the camera's WiFi access point is ready to accept client connections.

This status does not transition to `1` (true) immediately when [Set AP Control](/docs/ble/networking#set-ap-control) is sent
with mode=1 (on) or mode=2 (bounce). Instead, there is a brief delay after the command is accepted while the
camera starts the WiFi hardware and DHCP server. Poll or subscribe to this status and wait for it to become `1`
before attempting a WiFi connection to the camera's AP.

When the AP is disabled, this status transitions to `0` (false) immediately.

70.name: Internal Battery Percentage
70.description: Internal battery level as percentage (0–100). This is a more precise alternative to [Internal Battery Bars](/docs/ble/statuses#status-2) (Internal Battery Bars).

values.2: Accessory connected and a microphone is plugged into the accessory

78.name: Mobile Friendly
78.description: Are current video settings mobile friendly? (related to video compression and frame rate)

79.name: FTU
79.description: Is the camera currently in First Time Use (FTU) UI flow?

82.name: Ready
82.description: Is the system fully booted and ready to accept commands?

83.name: OTA Charged
83.description: Is the internal battery charged sufficiently to start Over The Air (OTA) update? Check this before initiating an OTA to avoid the update failing mid-way due to low battery.

85.name: Cold
85.description: Is the camera getting too cold to continue recording?

88.name: Zoom while Encoding
88.description: Is this camera model capable of zooming while encoding?

96.name: Preset Group
96.description: Current Preset Group (ID) (corresponds to ui_mode_groups in settings.json)

98.name: Preset Modified
98.description:
The value of this status is set to zero when the client sends a Get Preset Status message to the camera.

The value of this status is set to a non-zero value when:

- Preset settings submenu is exited in the camera UI (whether any settings were changed or not)
- A new preset is created
- A preset is deleted
- Preset ordering is changed within a preset group
- A preset is reset to factory defaults

99.name: Remaining Live Bursts
99.description: The number of Live Bursts can be captured with current settings before sdcard is full

100.name: Live Bursts
100.description: Total number of Live Bursts on sdcard

101.name: Capture Delay Active
101.description: Is Capture Delay currently active (i.e. counting down)?

104.name: Linux Core
104.description: Is the system's Linux core active?

105.name: Lens Type
105.description: Camera lens type (reflects changes to lens settings such as 162, 189, 194, ...)

110.name: Display Mod Status
110.description: Note that this is a bitmasked value.

values.0: 000 = Display Mod: 0, HDMI: 0, Display Mod Connected: False
values.1: 001 = Display Mod: 0, HDMI: 0, Display Mod Connected: True
values.2: 010 = Display Mod: 0, HDMI: 1, Display Mod Connected: False
values.3: 011 = Display Mod: 0, HDMI: 1, Display Mod Connected: True
values.4: 100 = Display Mod: 1, HDMI: 0, Display Mod Connected: False
values.5: 101 = Display Mod: 1, HDMI: 0, Display Mod Connected: True
values.6: 110 = Display Mod: 1, HDMI: 1, Display Mod Connected: False
values.7: 111 = Display Mod: 1, HDMI: 1, Display Mod Connected: True

111.name: SD Card Write Speed Error
111.description: Is there an SD Card minimum write speed error?

112.name: SD Card Errors
112.description: Number of sdcard write speed errors since device booted

values.0: Camera Idle: No one is attempting to change camera settings
values.1: Camera Control: Camera is in a menu or changing settings. To intervene, app must request control
values.2: Camera External Control: An outside entity (app) has control and is in a menu or modifying settings

115.name: USB Connected
115.description: Is the camera connected to a PC via USB?

117.name: SD Card Capacity
117.description: Total SD card capacity in Kilobytes

122.name: Camera Name
122.description: Custom camera name set by the user

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_NONE
values.description: No error (success)

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_NETWORK
values.description: General network error during the stream

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_CREATESTREAM
values.description: Startup error: bad URL or valid with live stream server

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_OUTOFMEMORY
values.description: Not enough memory on camera to complete task

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_INPUTSTREAM
values.description: Failed to get stream from low level camera system

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_INTERNET
values.description: No internet access detected on startup of streamer

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_OSNETWORK
values.description: Error occured in linux networking stack. Usually means the server closed the connection

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_SELECTEDNETWORKTIMEOUT
values.description: Timed out attemping to connect to the wifi network when attemping live stream

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_SSL_HANDSHAKE
values.description: SSL handshake failed (commonly caused due to incorrect time / time zone)

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_CAMERA_BLOCKED
values.description: Low level camera system rejected attempt to start live stream

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_UNKNOWN
values.description: Unknown

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_SD_CARD_FULL
values.description: Can not perform livestream because sd card is full

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_ERROR_SD_CARD_REMOVED
values.description: Livestream stopped because sd card was removed

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_IDLE
values.description: Initial status. Livestream has not yet been configured

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_CONFIG
values.description: Livestream is being configured

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_READY
values.description: Livestream has finished configuration and is ready to start streaming

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_STREAMING
values.description: Livestream is actively streaming

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_COMPLETE_STAY_ON
values.description: Live stream is exiting. No errors occured.

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_FAILED_STAY_ON
values.description: Live stream is exiting. An error occurred.

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_RECONNECTING
values.description: An error occurred during livestream and stream is attempting to reconnect.

values.name: LIVE_STREAM_STATE_UNAVAILABLE
values.description: Live stream setup is unavailable due to camera lens configuration

settings.json: HERO6..HERO12
Adds more video/photo metadata fields over MEDIA_METADATA_V4
https://goproinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SBO/pages/24016465/gpMediaMetadata

GPCAMERA_FEATURE_MEDIA_STABILIZATION_REQ.description: Flag to notify the app that media stabilization will have to be done on the app side

GPCAMERA_FEATURE_SHOW_HIDDEN_PRESETS.name: Feature Show Hidden Presets
GPCAMERA_FEATURE_SHOW_HIDDEN_PRESETS.description: Indicates that the `GetPresetStatus` operation supports the `include_hidden` parameter

GPCAMERA_FEATURE_GPMF_IN_VIDEO_THM_ID.name: Feature Gpmf In Video Thm ID
GPCAMERA_FEATURE_GPMF_IN_VIDEO_THM_ID.description: Feature flag to notify app and 3rd party that GPMF can be grabbed from video thumbnail

GPCAMERA_PREVIEW_ENABLED.name: Preview Enabled
GPCAMERA_PREVIEW_ENABLED.description: Legacy Preview. Only used on HERO3 (not supported by PyWSDK)

GPCAMERA_CANCEL_PAIR_WIRELESS_REMOTE_ID.name: Cancel Pair Wireless Remote ID
GPCAMERA_CANCEL_PAIR_WIRELESS_REMOTE_ID.description:
The presence of this feature flag indicates that the camera will cancel pairing if the wifi remote attempts to pair
when the camera was already paired.

GPCAMERA_WEBCAM.name: Webcam
GPCAMERA_WEBCAM.description:
This indicates that all of the following command ID's are supported_options:

- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_PREVIEW
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_START
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_STOP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_EXIT
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_STATUS
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_VERSION
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_PREVIEW_OGP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_START_OGP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_STOP_OGP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_EXIT_OGP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_STATUS_OGP
- GPCAMERA_WEBCAM_VERSION_OGP

GPCAMERA_GET_WIFI_CONFIG.name: Get WIFI Config
GPCAMERA_GET_WIFI_CONFIG.description: SSID and Password can be read with a single command.

GPCAMERA_MEDIA_METADATA_V4.name: Media Metadata V4
GPCAMERA_MEDIA_METADATA_V4.description:
Exists in HERO5..HERO12 (BOSS-3660)

Is not used by Android WSDK (see getMediaWithFields API in GpMediaGateway.java)

Never released: V4 = V1 + Session metadata (i.e. Karma)

Endpoint: /gp/gpMediaList?fields=xxx

Definition: MediaListV4 = MediaListV1 + Session files (media list + metadata)
References:
  https://goproinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SBO/pages/23986454/MediaList+V4+Australia
  https://goproinc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SBO/pages/24027744/GpControl+gpMediaList+Field+WhiteList

GPCAMERA_PRESET_DISPLAY_CAROUSEL.name: Preset Display Carousel
GPCAMERA_PRESET_DISPLAY_CAROUSEL.description: Feature flag to let WSDK know preset carousel UI should be displayed. This should indicate that only one mode group exists in the preset carousel.

GPCAMERA_FEATURE_MEDIA_OFFLOAD_WITHOUT_C.name: Feature Media Offload Without Charging
GPCAMERA_FEATURE_MEDIA_OFFLOAD_WITHOUT_C.description: Indicates the camera can upload to cloud without a charging cable connected
